ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bento class; no specific fried-chicken-and-takoyaki bento entry exists. Mapped to nearest bento ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers composite bento-style meals; no exact fried-chicken-and-takoyaki variant exists in the published hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for this specific composite bento meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bento box serving of approximately 380 g, comprising a portion of steamed white rice, 2–3 pieces of Japanese-style fried chicken, and 3 pieces of takoyaki with sauce and toppings.

nutrient_source.title: Fried chicken and takoyaki bento — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient record F05100056
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100g nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo record F05100056 (confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (fried chicken + takoyaki components) as HPB record does not report saturated fat separately; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite bento. GI estimated at 72 (high) based on white rice as the dominant carbohydrate component (GI ~72 per University of Sydney GI Database for white rice, boiled). Takoyaki batter and fried chicken coating contribute additional refined carbohydrate but are minor relative to rice volume. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 72 × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (72 × 83.2g carbohydrate in 380g serving) / 100 ≈ 30.0 g GL units. This is a high GL meal.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 286 mg/100g; full bento serving (~380g) delivers approximately 1,087 mg sodium — nearly half the WHO daily limit of 2,000 mg — with minimal potassium or dietary fibre to offset vascular sodium load.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this bento to a half-portion or less, request reduced or omitted takoyaki sauce and mayonnaise, and balance the meal with potassium-rich foods such as leafy greens or a banana to partially offset the high sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: White rice is the dominant component with a GI of approximately 72 (high); carbohydrate 21.9g/100g with only 0.88g dietary fibre per 100g results in rapid postprandial glucose elevation; full serving delivers approximately 83g available carbohydrate.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this bento due to its high glycaemic load (~30 GL per serving); if consumed, reduce the rice portion by half, substitute brown rice where possible, and pair with non-starchy vegetables and a protein source to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and oxidised lipids; estimated saturated fat ~1.2g/100g; sodium 286mg/100g; low dietary fibre (0.88g/100g) and absence of cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids make this a high-concern meal for CVD management.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this bento due to its combination of high sodium, deep-fried components with pro-inflammatory lipid oxidation products, and low fibre; if eaten, choose the smallest available portion and supplement with fibre-rich vegetables and a low-sodium beverage.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Both fried chicken and takoyaki are deep-fried, generating AGEs that promote joint inflammation; refined carbohydrate from white rice and batter contributes to inflammatory glycaemic load; no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this bento or consume it only occasionally in small portions, as the deep-fried components and refined carbohydrate base are pro-inflammatory; request additional vegetables and avoid takoyaki sauce and mayonnaise toppings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Chicken and Takoyaki Bento (フライドチキンとたこ焼き弁当) is a Japanese-style bento box meal combining Japanese-style fried chicken (similar to karaage — bite-sized chicken pieces coated in seasoned batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy) with three pieces of takoyaki (round wheat-flour dumplings filled with diced octopus, cooked in a specialised cast-iron mould, topped with takoyaki sauce, Japanese mayonnaise, bonito flakes, and aonori seaweed flakes), served alongside a portion of steamed white Japanese short-grain rice. The bento is a popular convenience-store and food-court format in Singapore and Japan, offering a complete one-box meal. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-moderate at 165 kcal/100g but carbohydrate-dominant (21.9g/100g) with a high glycaemic index (~72) driven by white rice, making it a high-glycaemic-load meal (~30 GL per standard 380g serving). Protein is meaningful at 9.43g/100g from chicken and octopus. Fat is moderate at 4.46g/100g, primarily from frying oil. Sodium is elevated at 286mg/100g, with a full serving delivering approximately 1,087mg sodium. Dietary fibre is low at 0.88g/100g. This meal is not suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without significant portion modification. It is generally considered halal when prepared with halal-certified chicken and sauces.
common_user_queries: Is fried chicken and takoyaki bento healthy?
How many calories in a takoyaki bento box?
Is this bento suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in a fried chicken bento?
Can I eat takoyaki bento if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of a bento with white rice?

confidence_reason: Core per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F05100056 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database white rice anchor (GI 72). Japanese local-language name verified. Ontology mapped to nearest defensible FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.2g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy for fried chicken and takoyaki components, as the HPB F05100056 record does not report saturated fat separately. This value should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite bento are not available in the HPB Foodo F05100056 record or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white rice (boiled, short-grain) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific bento product exists in the published literature. 4. The input record listed gi_value as 0 with confidence 0.328 from an 'Embedded GI Database' — this is not an approved GI source and has been replaced with the University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate. 5. Serving size of 380g is an estimate for a standard convenience-store bento box; actual product weight may vary. 6. Halal status is carried forward from the input record dietary_tags; this should be verified against the specific product's halal certification.
